US ECON DAILY REVIEW - Claims cool, KC Fed Mfg improves, Miran remains dove
relatedHeadlines.snippet: We only had a couple of data releases today, with initial claims lower than expected and a decent improvement in the Kansas City Fed Manufacturing Survey.

data.text: COMMENT: NVDA beats again, AI bubble concerns persist
data.snippet: NVDA Q4 earnings were released after the bell on Wednesday and once again they beat expectations with record revenue and net profits.

image.text: An Nvidia logo appears in this illustration created on August 25, 2025.

data.text: COMMENT: PBoC - Yuan breaks 6.85, growing divergence to fix
data.snippet: It was at the end of 2025 that USD/CNY broke 7.00 and in 2-months the unit has already surpassed 6.85. These are big moves for the yuan which tends to move gradually.

image.text: Chinese 100 yuan banknotes are seen in this picture illustration created in Shanghai on January 17 , 2011.

data.text: COMMENT: Reflexivity & the 2028 AI-driven economic crisis thought experiment
data.snippet: A report on an AI-driven economic crisis in the year 2028 is creating a lot of attention and debate. The report, from Citrini Research, is a thought-experiment written from the perspective of 2028 looking at what happens in a world where AI adoption succeeds at a faster than expected pace.

image.text: Figurines with computers and smartphones are seen in front of the words "Artificial Intelligence AI" in this illustration created on February 19, 2024.

data.text: MBS MIDDAY – Lagging as markets flip to risk-off after Nvidia fails to ease AI concerns
data.snippet: The basis was lagging at the open, with Treasuries seeing another very tight range (1.9bp on 10s) overnight and equity index futures little changed in the Nasdaq100 to higher.

image.text: People walk around the Financial District on the morning after Zohran Mamdani’s election win in New York City, U.S., November 5, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: MBS - Preview of Friday's data and events
relatedHeadlines.snippet: PPI (Jan) The outlook on the January PPI report is cautiously optimistic, with the growth in prices expected to be marginal.

data.text: Nippon Steel raises ¥600bn from Japan’s largest CB
data.snippet: Nippon Steel has raised ¥600bn (US$3.84bn) from an upsized dual-tranche convertible bond to repay part of the bridge loans incurred to finance last year's acquisition of US Steel.

image.text: The Nippon Steel logo is displayed at the company's East Nippon Works Kimitsu Area plant in Kimitsu, east of Tokyo, Japan May 26, 2025.

data.text: Tariffs fail to unnerve European lev loan market
data.snippet: The latest round of tariff rhetoric and manoeuvring from the Trump administration has failed to unnerve a European leveraged loan market that has become somewhat numb to the noise.

image.text: Shipping containers are shown stacked at the West Basin Container Terminal at the Port of Los Angeles in Los Angeles, California, U.S., February 24,  2026.

data.text: EUR GOVTS CLOSE: Nvidia slide caps otherwise boring day
data.snippet: Bunds did next to nothing almost all day, holding within a slim 129.57 to 129.69 range, before jumping with Treasuries as the Nasdaq is sliding into Europe’s close.

image.text: German share price index DAX graph is pictured at the stock exchange in Frankfurt

relatedHeadlines.text: Modest concession for BTPs before month-end auctions
relatedHeadlines.snippet: BTPs are underperforming modestly, putting in a marginal concession before this morning’s €5.5bn-€6.5bn of taps of the 2/31 and 2/36 maturities alongside €2.0bn-€2.5bn of the 4/35 floating rate CCTeu.

data.snippet: Luxury retailer Saks Global has obtained court approval for a US$1bn debtor-in-possession new money loans as it navigates its Chapter 11 bankruptcy process.

image.text: The Saks Fifth Avenue flagship store, after the store filed for bankruptcy protection, in New York City, U.S., January 14, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: Metropolitan fuels Florida expansion with US$178.5m raise
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Metropolitan Bank secured US$178.5m late Wednesday from a follow-on sale of stock to fund expansion, opting to confidentially market the financing ahead of overnight execution to minimize market risk.

relatedHeadlines.text: KDP to back JDE Peet's buyout with US$9bn debt
Orlen pushes for US$1.4bn Energa rights issue
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Polish electricity company Energa has received a proposal from major shareholder Orlen for a Z5.11bn (US$1.43bn) rights issue following Orlen's attempt last year to take full ownership of the business.

relatedHeadlines.text: Accell's lenders discuss sale
Inetum added to restructuring watchlist

data.text: Lloyds RMBS highlights strong demand for Dutch collateral
data.snippet: The reception of Lloyds Bank’s latest Dutch RBMS underscored that there is sustained investor appetite for Dutch collateral and the broader positive tone surrounding European securitisations.

image.text: People walk past a branch of Lloyds Bank in London, Britain, January 28, 2026.

data.text: SAR oversubscribed on large retail tranche
data.snippet: Saudi cement producer Saleh Abdulaziz Al Rashed & Sons Company attracted SR121.5m (US$32.4m) of demand for the 30% retail tranche of its SR251.1m IPO.

image.text: A Saudi money exchanger wears gloves as he counts Saudi riyal currency at a currency exchange shop in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia March 10, 2020.

data.text: Indonesia returns for Rmb, euro bonds
data.snippet: The Republic of Indonesia continued its borrowing spree for the year with a Rmb9.25bn (US$1.3bn) triple-tranche Dim Sum deal and a €2.7bn (US$3.2bn) triple-tranche portion printed on Wednesday.

image.text: An Indonesia Rupiah note is seen in this picture illustration June 2, 2017.

relatedHeadlines.text: Ontario takes record euro size
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Ontario has priced its biggest ever euro benchmark with its new €3bn 10-year.

data.text: JPN GOVTS CLOSE: Futures off on hawkish BoJ comments, long-end bonds steady
data.snippet: Commentary JGBs softened on hawkish comments from Bank of Japan governor Kauo Ueda despite prime minister Sanae Takaich's reservations about more rate increases.

image.text: A Japanese flag flutters atop the Bank of Japan headquarters in Tokyo, Japan, December19, 2025.

data.snippet: American Honda Finance has raised US$8.5bn of revolving credit facilities comprising a 364-day, a three-year and a five-year RCF, each totalling US$2.83bn.

relatedHeadlines.text: First Solar gets RCF
MSI welcomed to euro market
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Mitsui Sumitomo Insurance Company drummed up more than €2.65bn of demand for its inaugural euro-denominated senior unsecured transaction on Thursday.

data.text: Vishal Mega Mart selldown to raise Rs35bn
data.snippet: Controlling shareholder Samayat Services is selling part of its stake in India's Vishal Mega Mart to raise Rs35bn (US$386m).

relatedHeadlines.text: MCB taps GCC and India
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Mauritius Commercial Bank has closed an inaugural Gulf Cooperation Council and India-focused syndicated term loan for US$450m, further diversifying the bank’s international lender base and expanding its relationships in key global markets.

relatedHeadlines.text: Vedanta returns to bond market for up to Rs30bn
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Vedanta Limited is planning to raise up to Rs30bn (US$330m) from rupee bonds after it received board approval on February 25, according to market sources.
